# criblio_search_saved_query (Resource)

SearchSavedQuery Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_search_saved_query" "my_search_saved_query" {
  description = "Saved search for recent Windows security channel errors."
  earliest = "-24h"
  id = "saved_winlog_errors"
  is_private = true
  latest = "now"
  name = "Windows security errors"
  query = "dataset=\"windows_events\" | limit 200"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the saved search.
- `name` (String) Display name for the saved search.
- `query` (String) Query text for the saved search.

### Optional

- `chart_config` (Attributes) Chart configuration for scheduled or Dashboard-backed runs. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config))
- `description` (String) Brief description of the saved search.
- `earliest` (String) Start of the search time range. Accepts a relative time string or an absolute timestamp.
- `is_private` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, job results are restricted to the owner.
- `latest` (String) End of the search time range. Accepts a relative time string or an absolute timestamp.
- `sample_rate` (Number) Sampling rate that is applied to events, between 0 and 1.
- `schedule` (Attributes) Cron schedule and Notification settings for the saved search when it runs on a schedule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--schedule))
- `timezone` (String) IANA timezone for scheduled execution.
- `user` (String) Identifier for the user who created the saved search. Scheduled runs execute as the specified user.

### Read-Only

- `display_username` (String) Display name for the user who created the saved search.
- `is_system` (Boolean)
- `lib` (String) Library scope for the saved search. Indicates whether the definition is built-in or custom.
- `resolved_dataset_ids` (List of String) Dataset identifiers that are resolved from the query for role-based access control (RBAC) and scheduling.
- `search_job_source` (String) Source context that created the job.
- `table_config` (Map of String) Table layout configuration.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config`

Optional:

- `apply_threshold` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, apply color thresholds to the chart values.
- `axis` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--axis))
- `color` (String) CSS color value for single-value counters.
- `color_palette` (Integer) Index of the color palette to apply to the chart.
- `color_palette_reversed` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, reverse the color palette order.
- `color_thresholds` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--color_thresholds))
- `custom_data` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--custom_data))
- `decimals` (Integer) Number of decimal places for numeric display.
- `label` (String) Display label for single-value counters.
- `legend` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--legend))
- `map_details` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--map_details))
- `on_click_action` (Attributes) Action to perform when a user clicks on the chart. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--on_click_action))
- `prefix` (String) String prepended to the displayed value.
- `separator` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, apply thousands separators to numeric values.
- `series` (Attributes List) Explicit series definitions with individual styling. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--series))
- `series_info` (Map of String) Map of series names to display overrides (name, color, type, area style).
- `should_apply_user_chart_settings` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, apply user-level chart display preferences.
- `style` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, apply custom styling to the chart.
- `suffix` (String) String appended to the displayed value.
- `type` (String) Default chart type for all series.
- `x_axis` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--x_axis))
- `y_axis` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--y_axis))

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--axis"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.axis`

Optional:

- `x_axis` (String) Field name for the X-axis.
- `y_axis` (List of String) Field names for the Y-axis.
- `y_axis_excluded` (List of String) Field names excluded from the Y-axis.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--color_thresholds"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.color_thresholds`

Required:

- `thresholds` (Attributes List) Ordered list of threshold breakpoints with associated colors. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--color_thresholds--thresholds))

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--color_thresholds--thresholds"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.color_thresholds.thresholds`

Required:

- `color` (String) CSS color applied when the value meets or exceeds the <code>threshold</code>.
- `threshold` (Number) Numeric threshold value.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--custom_data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.custom_data`

Optional:

- `connect_nulls` (String) Strategy for connecting null data points.
- `data_fields` (List of String) Specific data fields to include.
- `is_point_color` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, apply color encoding to data points.
- `limit_to_top_n` (Integer) Limit the display to the top N series by value.
- `lines` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, render connecting lines between data points.
- `name_field` (String) Field name used for data point labels.
- `point_color_palette` (Integer) Index of the color palette used for point coloring.
- `point_color_palette_reversed` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, reverse the point color palette order.
- `point_scale` (String) Scale factor for scatter plot points.
- `point_scale_data_field` (String) Field name whose values determine point size.
- `series_count` (Integer) Maximum number of series to display.
- `split_by` (String) Field name to split series by.
- `stack` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, stack series on top of each other.
- `summarize_others` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, aggregate remaining series into an 'Others' bucket.
- `trellis` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, render each series in its own small-multiples panel.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--legend"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.legend`

Optional:

- `position` (String) Legend position.
- `selected` (Map of String) Map of series names to their visibility state in the legend.
- `truncate` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, truncate long legend labels.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--map_details"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.map_details`

Optional:

- `latitude_field` (String) Field name for latitude values.
- `longitude_field` (String) Field name for longitude values.
- `map_source_id` (String) Identifier of the map source.
- `map_type` (String) Map projection type.
- `name_field` (String) Field name for region names on the map.
- `point_scale` (String) Scale factor for map data points.
- `value_field` (String) Field name for the data value driving map coloring or sizing.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--on_click_action"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.on_click_action`

Optional:

- `search` (String) Search query string to execute on click.
- `selected_dashboard_id` (String) The <code>id</code> of the target Dashboard for drilldown navigation.
- `selected_input_id` (String) The <code>id</code> of the Input element whose value is passed to the target Dashboard.
- `selected_link_id` (String) The <code>id</code> of the link element for external URL navigation.
- `selected_timerange_input_id` (String) The <code>id</code> of the time range Input element whose value is passed to the target Dashboard.
- `type` (String) Action type.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--series"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.series`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the data series, displayed in legends and tooltips.

Optional:

- `area_style` (Attributes) Area fill style options for area charts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--series--area_style))
- `color` (String) CSS color value for the series.
- `data` (List of String) Array of data points for the series.
- `map` (String) Map identifier for geo-chart series.
- `type` (String) Chart type override for the individual series.
- `y_axis_field` (String) Field name from the data to use for the Y-axis values.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--series--area_style"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.series.area_style`

Optional:

- `opacity` (Number) Opacity of the area fill, from 0 (transparent) to 1 (opaque).
- `shadow_blur` (Integer) Blur radius of the shadow in pixels.
- `shadow_color` (String) CSS color of the shadow.
- `shadow_offset_x` (Integer) Horizontal offset of the shadow in pixels.
- `shadow_offset_y` (Integer) Vertical offset of the shadow in pixels.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--x_axis"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.x_axis`

Optional:

- `data_field` (String) Field name for the X-axis data.
- `format` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--x_axis--format))
- `inverse` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, reverse the axis direction.
- `label_interval` (String) Interval strategy for axis label spacing.
- `label_orientation` (Integer) Label rotation angle in degrees.
- `name` (String) Display name for the axis.
- `offset` (Integer) Pixel offset of the axis from its default position.
- `position` (String) Axis position.
- `type` (String) Axis type.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--x_axis--format"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.x_axis.format`

Required:

- `format` (String) Numeric format token. Unknown tokens are rendered as-is.

- `prefix` (String) String prepended to the formatted numeric value.
- `suffix` (String) String appended to the formatted numeric value.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--y_axis"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.y_axis`

Optional:

- `data_field` (List of String) Field names for the Y-axis data.
- `format` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--chart_config--y_axis--format))
- `interval` (Number) Fixed interval between axis tick marks.
- `max` (Number) Maximum value for the axis range.
- `min` (Number) Minimum value for the axis range.
- `position` (String) Axis position.
- `scale` (String) Scale type.
- `split_line` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, show horizontal grid lines.
- `type` (String) Axis type.

<a id="nestedatt--chart_config--y_axis--format"></a>
### Nested Schema for `chart_config.y_axis.format`

Required:

- `format` (String) Numeric format token. Unknown tokens are rendered as-is.

Optional:

- `prefix` (String) String prepended to the formatted numeric value.
- `suffix` (String) String appended to the formatted numeric value.

<a id="nestedatt--schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule`

Required:

- `cron_schedule` (String) Unix cron expression (5 fields) that defines when the scheduled queries run.
- `enabled` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, the schedule is active and the Dashboard queries run automatically at the specified <code>cronSchedule</code>.
- `keep_last_n` (Integer) Minimum number of past execution artifacts to retain. Older artifacts are purged periodically. Default is <code>2</code>.
- `tz` (String) IANA timezone identifier for the cron schedule.

Optional:

- `notifications` (Attributes) Notification settings for scheduled query executions. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--schedule--notifications))

<a id="nestedatt--schedule--notifications"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule.notifications`

Required:

- `disabled` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, Notifications for the scheduled query are disabled. Default is <code>false</code> (Notifications enabled).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_search_saved_query.my_criblio_search_saved_query
  id = "sq-errors"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_search_saved_query.my_criblio_search_saved_query sq-errors
```
